title: Americans Keep Spending, but Watch Prices

source.title: Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan on how Americans are spending their money

segments.title: Trading Down
segments.audioText: Higher end pet food is not being charged as much on our credit and debit cards as the next brand down.
segments.transcript: Higher end pet food is not being charged as much on our credit and debit cards as the next brand down.
segments.translationZh: 高價寵物食品喺我哋信用卡同扣帳卡上嘅消費，已經冇下一級品牌咁多。

points.label: Higher end pet food
points.explanationZh: higher end 即較高檔、較貴；pet food 係寵物食品，先成組聽。

points.label: is not being charged as much
points.explanationZh: 呢度 charged 指用卡付款；not as much 表示『冇咁多』。

points.label: the next brand down
points.explanationZh: brand down 指低一級或平一級嘅品牌，講消費者轉買較便宜選擇。

segments.title: Vacations and Restaurants
segments.audioText: Consumers are still spending on vacations and things like that, which is good for America.
segments.transcript: Consumers are still spending on vacations and things like that, which is good for America.
segments.translationZh: 消費者仍然會花錢去旅行之類，呢個對美國嚟講係好事。

points.label: Consumers are still spending
points.explanationZh: still spending 表示『仍然有消費』，新聞講經濟時好常見。

points.label: on vacations and things like that
points.explanationZh: spend on 後面接花錢嘅項目；things like that 係口語『之類』。

points.label: which is good for America
points.explanationZh: which 回指前面整件事，補充佢認為呢種消費對經濟有利。

segments.title: Watch Behavior
segments.audioText: We watch what they do, not what they say.
segments.transcript: We watch what they do, not what they say.
segments.translationZh: 我哋睇嘅係佢哋實際做咩，而唔係佢哋講咩。

points.label: We watch
points.explanationZh: watch 呢度唔係睇電視，而係『觀察、留意』。

points.label: what they do
points.explanationZh: what they do 係名詞子句，意思係『佢哋做嘅事』。

points.label: not what they say
points.explanationZh: 用 not... 對比行為同說法，句型短但好實用。

segments.title: Prices and Affordability
segments.audioText: What they're saying is they're very upset and they're worried about high prices, affordability.
segments.transcript: What they're saying is they're very upset and they're worried about high prices, affordability.
segments.translationZh: 佢哋口頭上講嘅係，佢哋好唔滿，亦擔心高物價同負擔能力。

points.label: What they're saying is
points.explanationZh: 開頭用 what...is 帶出重點，聽到 is 後面就係核心內容。

points.label: they're very upset
points.explanationZh: upset 可以係唔開心、嬲、擔心，視乎語境；呢度係對物價不滿。

points.label: high prices, affordability
points.explanationZh: affordability 指『負擔得起嘅程度』，同 high prices 經常一齊出現。

sentences: Higher end pet food is not being charged as much on our credit and debit cards as the next brand down.
Consumers are still spending on vacations and things like that, which is good for America.
We watch what they do, not what they say.
What they're saying is they're very upset and they're worried about high prices, affordability.

vocabulary.example: Consumers are watching prices more carefully.
vocabulary.translationZh: 消費者而家更小心留意價錢。

vocabulary.example: Travel spending went up this month.
vocabulary.translationZh: 今個月旅遊消費上升咗。

vocabulary.example: They are saving money for a vacation.
This brand is a little cheaper.
Affordability is a big concern for families.
vocabulary.translationZh: 負擔能力係好多家庭好關心嘅事。

vocabulary.example: People are upset about higher prices.

phrases.example: Consumers are still spending on vacations.
We need snacks, drinks, and things like that.

phrases.phrase: what they do, not what they say
phrases.meaningZh: 睇佢哋做咩，而唔係講咩
phrases.example: In business, watch what people do, not what they say.
Many families are worried about high prices.

speakingPractice.instructionZh: 由 00:55 跟讀 Consumers are still spending...，留意 spending on 要連住講。
重播 01:14，聽寫 We watch what they do, not what they say. 呢句短句。
用 things like that 練替換：coffee, snacks, and things like that。
用兩句英文複述：Consumers are still spending. But many people are worried about high prices.

notes: 今日重點係商業新聞入面好貼近日常生活嘅字：consumers、spending、prices、affordability。
時間碼 00:26、00:55、01:14、01:23 可以逐句重播；先聽數次，再看稿跟讀。
We watch what they do, not what they say. 係好實用嘅對比句型，可以搬去工作同生活場景。
dailyLifePractice 會配合消費主題，練習問價錢、控制預算、同付款時自然溝通。

dailyLifePractice.title: Checking a Price
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你喺超市或店入面見到標價唔清楚，想自然問店員確認。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Hi, can you check the price on this for me? I thought it was on sale. No worries, I'll still take it.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Hi, can you check the price on this for me?
lines.zh: 你好，可唔可以幫我查一查呢個價錢？

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I thought it was on sale.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: No worries, I'll still take it.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: Can you check the price on this for me? 好自然又有禮貌。No worries 可以令語氣輕鬆，唔會似投訴。

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 price 換成 size、discount 或 total。
drills.example: Can you check the discount on this for me?

drills.label: Quick repeat
drills.prompt: 跟讀 I thought it was on sale 三次，留意 thought it 連讀。
drills.example: I thought it was on sale.

dailyLifePractice.title: Keeping It Under Budget
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你同朋友買午餐，想講自己今日想慳少少，但語氣自然唔尷尬。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: I'm trying to keep lunch under fifteen today. That place is a little pricey. Can we grab something quick instead?

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.en: Do you want to try that new sandwich place?
lines.zh: 你想唔想試嗰間新三文治店？

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I'm trying to keep lunch under fifteen today.
lines.zh: 我今日想將午餐控制喺十五蚊以下。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: That place is a little pricey.

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.en: Sure, let's grab something quick instead.
lines.zh: 可以呀，咁我哋買啲快手嘅嘢啦。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: a little pricey 比 too expensive 婉轉，聽落冇咁硬。keep lunch under fifteen 係好地道嘅預算講法。

drills.label: Say-it-your-way
drills.prompt: 用 keep...under... 講一個你嘅預算。
drills.example: I'm trying to keep dinner under twenty tonight.

drills.label: Polite rephrase
drills.prompt: 將 too expensive 改成較自然嘅講法。
drills.example: It's a little pricey for me.

dailyLifePractice.title: Payment Question
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你付款時想問可唔可以分開付，或者用另一張卡，簡短清楚就得。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Can we split the payment? I'll put ten on this card and the rest on my debit card. Thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Cashier
lines.en: Sure. How would you like to split it?

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I'll put ten on this card.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: And the rest on my debit card, please.
lines.zh: 其餘用我張扣帳卡，唔該。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: put ten on this card 係收銀位好常聽到嘅講法，意思係『呢張卡扣十蚊』。the rest 即剩低嗰部分。

drills.label: Role-play
drills.prompt: 你做顧客，講一句分開付款。

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 ten 換成 five、twenty 或 half。
drills.example: I'll put twenty on this card.
